title: pittosporaceae
intro: Pittosporaceae is a family of flowering plants, commonly known as the forget-me-not family. It comprises approximately 270 species of trees, shrubs, and climbing plants, native to various parts of the world, including the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia, Australia, and the Pacific Islands. The plants within this family are often characterized by their attractive flowers, which can range in color from white and yellow to blue and purple, and their small, usually fragrant fruits. Many species of Pittosporaceae are valued for their ornamental and timber values, and some are also used in traditional medicine.
